Reasonable for: (1) Open GIMP, (2-3) Navigate Layer→Transform→Offset menu, (4-6) Set X/Y offset values, (7) Select wrap-around mode, (8) Apply offset. Consistent with other multi-parameter dialog tasks.
All tasks in gimp_env_all use sparse rewards (only pass/fail feedback, no intermediate rewards)

pi_items.metadata_value: Layer → Transform → Offset (as stated in README)
pi_items.source: README section 2 and instructions in setup_offset_task.sh line 37-38 specify this exact menu path. GIMP official documentation confirms this is the correct path.

pi_items.verified_value: 'Wrap around' mode is correctly distinguished from background fill in verifier
pi_items.source: Verifier.py check_wrap_around_mode() (lines 78-105) detects background fill by monitoring black/white pixel increase. Wrap-around uses numpy.roll() which correctly implements modular wraparound.
Verified in verifier.py line 246: 'if ssim_score >= 0.95'. Tested with reference offset image which produces SSIM=1.0 with itself.

pi_items.metadata_value: Monitor black/white pixel increase to distinguish wrap-around from background fill
pi_items.verified_value: Threshold: black_increase_ratio < 0.05 AND white_increase_ratio < 0.05 (less than 5% increase)
pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 102-103: 'wrap_around_used = black_increase_ratio < 0.05 and white_increase_ratio < 0.05'. Tested with reference offset which shows 0% increase in black/white pixels, correctly detecting wrap-around.

pi_items.metadata_value: >= 60% of edge samples should show continuity
pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 161-162. This checks if at least 60% of sampled edge pixels match expected source pixels within threshold of 30 pixel-value difference.

pi_items.metadata_value: 5 criteria total: (1) SSIM>=0.95, (2) Wrap-around mode, (3) Boundary continuity, (4) Dimensions preserved, (5) Image modified
pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 243-255 enumerate all 5 criteria and score based on count: (criteria_met / 5) * 100
verifier.py line 259: 'passed = score >= 75'. This means agent must meet at least 4 out of 5 criteria (80% score) or have sufficiently high partial scores.

pi_items.metadata_value: Uses numpy.roll() to programmatically apply offset with wrap-around
pi_items.verified_value: numpy.roll() correctly implements wrap-around: np.roll(..., shift=offset_y, axis=0) then np.roll(..., shift=offset_x, axis=1)
pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 33-34. Tested: verified that pixels at (0,0) come from (height-offset_y, width-offset_x) with modular wrap, proving correctness.

pi_items.metadata_value: ImageMagick fallback if downloads fail: 'convert -size 400x300 -define gradient:direction=NorthWest gradient:red-blue -swirl 50'
pi_items.verified_value: Fallback present in setup_offset_task.sh lines 17-19 with exact command
pi_items.source: Code inspection of setup_offset_task.sh. Fallback creates synthetic 400x300 image if both Unsplash URLs fail.

privileged_info_summary: The offset_wrap task requires applying a 100px horizontal and 80px vertical offset with wrap-around mode to an image in GIMP. The primary data source is Unsplash (real image data: 800x600 JPEG), with a fallback to synthetic ImageMagick-generated patterns. The verifier uses numpy.roll() to generate a reference offset image and compares results using SSIM>=0.95. Wrap-around mode is correctly distinguished from background fill by monitoring black/white pixel increases (must be <5%). The task requires 4 out of 5 criteria to pass (80% score). All specified values (timeout=120s, max_steps=8, offsets=100x80px) are correct and reasonable. The verifier implementation is mathematically sound for detecting properly applied offset transformations.
